scored_claims.claim: CA Technologies' strategic separation of platinum accounts (large ELA renewals) from named and growth accounts (new logos and expansion), combined with product discipline specialization in the sales force, has improved consistent execution—evidenced by EMEA growing for 3 consecutive quarters by Q3 FY2015 and Enterprise Solutions declining from -20% to approaching flat over 2 fiscal years.
scored_claims.evidence: Q3 FY2015 call: 'EMEA team's third quarter in a row of performance that was in line with our expectations.' CEO: 'Enterprise segment was shrinking by 20% [when he joined]; After 1 year, we cut that in half. If you take a look at where we'll end this year, Enterprise segment will be down to flat.' Q3 FY2014 call: CEO described new quota-bearing rep structure and account separation strategy. By Q2 FY2016: 'improved win rates, decreased average sales cycles, improved pipeline conversion rates and increased average deal sizes.'
scored_claims.scoring_rationale: Sales execution improvements are operationally important and show management quality, but do not constitute a structural competitive advantage per 7 Powers framework. Rule08 applied. 30% confidence.

scored_claims.claim: CA Technologies maintained a non-GAAP operating margin of 37-39% throughout FY2014 (raised midpoint guidance to 37%) and FY2015 (guidance maintained at 37%), with Mainframe segment margin consistently 58-62%, and CFFO guidance of $960M-$1.04B (FY2014) and $1.0B-$1.07B (FY2015), demonstrating sustained cash generation despite flat-to-declining revenue.
scored_claims.evidence: Q3 FY2014 call: 'Raising full year non-GAAP operating margins to 37% from 36%.' Mainframe margin 62%. Full year CFFO guidance $960M-$1.04B. Q3 FY2015 call: 'Non-GAAP operating margin of 37%.' Mainframe margin 58%. CFFO guidance $1.0B-$1.07B. CFO noted this includes headwinds from tax normalization and restructuring.
scored_claims.scoring_rationale: Consistent 37%+ non-GAAP operating margins and $1B+ CFFO despite revenue headwinds demonstrate the resilience of CA's subscription-based model. The mainframe moat sustains cash generation even as Enterprise Solutions transitions. 70% confidence for cagr_connection.

scored_claims.claim: CA Technologies reported Q3 FY2014 revenue of $1.16B (down 2%), non-GAAP operating margin of 39%, and raised FY2014 full-year non-GAAP operating margin guidance to 37%. Q3 FY2015 revenue was $1.09B (down 1%), non-GAAP operating margin of 38%, with full-year FY2015 CFFO guidance of $1.0B-$1.07B. Mainframe segment margins were 62% (Q3 FY2014) and 58% (Q3 FY2015).
scored_claims.evidence: Directly reported in Q3 FY2014 and Q3 FY2015 earnings call transcripts. These are reported financial results and management-issued guidance.
scored_claims.scoring_rationale: Directly reported financial results. 90% confidence for factual baseline. Note: CA Technologies has a March 31 fiscal year; 'Q3' calls occur in January and reflect year-to-date results through December.
